subject: Gaming
title: PlayStation Changes the Game
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: PlayStation Changes the Game.

hook.text: Sony had never made a game console. In 1994, the PlayStation launched and rewrote the entire industry.

buildup.text: PlayStation used CDs instead of cartridges—cheaper to produce and capable of holding far more data.

discovery.text: Sony targeted adults with mature games like Resident Evil and Tomb Raider. Gaming wasn't just for kids.

twist.text: PlayStation was born from a failed Nintendo partnership. Sony built an empire from revenge.

climax.text: The PS1 sold 102 million units and ended Nintendo's dominance of the console market.

punchline.text: Nintendo rejected Sony. Sony built an empire from the rejection.

quiz.question: How did the PlayStation originate?
options: From a failed partnership between Sony and Nintendo
It was originally a music player
